queries.name: GetAllPredictions
queries.query: SELECT ... FROM fraud_predictions ORDER BY created_at DESC

queries.name: GetDashboardSummary
queries.query: SELECT COUNT(*) ... FROM fraud_predictions (with FILTER aggregations)

queries.name: GetFraudTrend
queries.query: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at), COUNT(*) FROM fraud_predictions GROUP BY ...

queries.name: RecentTransactions
queries.query: SELECT * FROM transactions ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20

queries.name: PendingTransactions (Risk Queue)
queries.query: SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE status = 'PENDING' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20

missing_index_recommendations.rationale: GetAllPredictions ORDER BY and GetFraudTrend GROUP BY both sort on created_at via quicksort (1394kB and 745kB). An index would enable index-only scan eliminating the sort step entirely. Critical as rows grow beyond 100K.
missing_index_recommendations.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_fraud_predictions_created_at ON fraud_predictions (created_at DESC);

missing_index_recommendations.rationale: RecentTransactions query sorts via Top-N Heapsort. An index would provide sorted scan without sort step.
missing_index_recommendations.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_created_at ON transactions (created_at DESC);

missing_index_recommendations.rationale: GetPredictionByTransactionID filters 9222 out of 9223 rows via Seq Scan. A btree index would reduce to a single index lookup (< 0.1ms). Currently acceptable at 9K rows, critical at scale.
PendingTransactions filters by status='PENDING' then sorts by created_at. Composite index would enable both filter and sort in one index scan.
missing_index_recommendations.sql: CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_status_created_at ON transactions (status, created_at DESC);

missing_index_recommendations.rationale: GetDashboardSummary aggregates COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE decision = 'REVIEW'/'ALLOW'). A partial index on decision could help, but aggregate over 9K rows is only 2.4ms.
